results.question: Can my bank charge a prepayment penalty on my floating rate home loan?
results.expected_answer: No, banks cannot charge foreclosure charges or pre-payment penalties on floating rate home loans to individual borrowers as per RBI guidelines.
results.actual_answer: No, your bank cannot charge a prepayment penalty or foreclosure charges on your floating rate home loan. This is mandated by RBI guidelines and applies to individual borrowers, regardless of how you fund the prepayment. If a bank imposes such charges, it violates RBI directives, and you can lodge a complaint with the Banking Ombudsman.

results.question: What is the maximum LTV ratio for a home loan of Rs. 50 lakhs?
results.expected_answer: For loans above Rs. 30 lakhs and up to Rs. 75 lakhs, the LTV ratio shall not exceed 80%.
results.actual_answer: For a home loan of Rs. 50 lakhs, the maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio shall not exceed 80%.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: What is the LTV ratio for a home loan of Rs. 25 lakhs?
results.expected_answer: For loans up to Rs. 30 lakhs, the LTV ratio shall not exceed 90%.
results.actual_answer: For a home loan of Rs. 25 lakhs, the Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio shall not exceed 90%.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: What is the foreclosure charge for a fixed rate home loan?
results.expected_answer: For fixed rate loans, banks may levy a foreclosure charge not exceeding 2% of the outstanding principal at the time of foreclosure.
results.actual_answer: For fixed rate loans, banks can charge a foreclosure fee of up to 2% of the outstanding principal at the time of foreclosure. This charge must be clearly stated in the loan agreement and the Key Fact Statement (KFS) provided when the loan is sanctioned.

results.question: Within how many days must a bank resolve a customer complaint?
results.expected_answer: Customer complaints must be acknowledged within 3 working days and resolved within 30 days.
results.actual_answer: Banks must resolve customer complaints within 30 days.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: What are the priority sector lending targets for domestic banks?
results.expected_answer: Domestic commercial banks must maintain 40% of ANBC as priority sector lending, with sub-targets of Agriculture: 18%, Micro Enterprises: 7.5%, Weaker Sections: 12%.
results.actual_answer: Domestic commercial banks are required to maintain 40% of Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) or Credit Equivalent of Off-Balance Sheet Exposures (CEOBE), whichever is higher, as priority sector lending.

results.question: How many days notice must I give to prepay my home loan?
results.expected_answer: The borrower must give 7 days written notice to the Lender specifying the amount to prepay.
results.actual_answer: To prepay your home loan, you must give 7 days' written notice to the Lender.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: What happens if the bank does not release title documents after full repayment?
results.expected_answer: The Lender shall release the title documents within 15 working days of full repayment.
results.actual_answer: The provided context states that upon full repayment, the Lender shall release the title documents within 15 working days and cancel the mortgage charge. However, it does not specify what happens if the bank does not release the title documents after full repayment.

results.question: Within how many days must a bank register mortgage charge with CERSAI?
results.expected_answer: The Lender shall register the mortgage charge with CERSAI within 30 days.
results.actual_answer: A bank must register the mortgage charge with CERSAI within 30 days.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: What is the maximum processing fee a bank can charge on a retail loan?
results.expected_answer: Processing fees shall not exceed 1% of the loan amount for retail loans.
results.actual_answer: For retail loans, banks cannot charge processing fees that are more than 1% of the loan amount.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: What is the maximum waiting period for pre-existing diseases in health insurance?
results.expected_answer: The maximum waiting period for pre-existing diseases shall not exceed 36 months from the date of first policy inception.
results.actual_answer: The maximum waiting period for pre-existing diseases (PED) in health insurance cannot be more than 36 months from the date the policy first started.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: What is the initial waiting period in a health insurance policy?
results.expected_answer: All health insurance policies must have an initial waiting period of 30 days from the date of commencement.
results.actual_answer: All health insurance policies must have an initial waiting period of 30 days from the policy's start date. During this period, no claims are payable except for accidental injuries. This waiting period does not apply if you port your policy.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: How many days before renewal must I apply for health insurance portability?
results.expected_answer: Portability request must be made at least 45 days before the renewal date.
results.actual_answer: You must make your portability request at least 45 days before your health insurance policy's renewal date.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: Within how many hours must cashless health insurance claims be settled?
results.expected_answer: Insurers must settle cashless claims within 3 hours of receiving the final request from the hospital.
results.actual_answer: Insurers must settle cashless health insurance claims within 3 hours of receiving the final request from the hospital.
failed_rules: length_reasonable

results.question: What is the free look period for a term insurance policy?
results.expected_answer: The policyholder has a free look period of 30 days from the date of receipt of the policy document.

results.question: क्या बैंक मेरे फ्लोटिंग रेट होम लोन पर प्री-पेमेंट चार्ज लगा सकता है?
results.expected_answer: नहीं, RBI के दिशानिर्देशों के अनुसार बैंक फ्लोटिंग रेट होम लोन पर प्री-पेमेंट पेनल्टी नहीं लगा सकते।
results.actual_answer: I don't have enough information to answer this reliably.

results.question: स्वास्थ्य बीमा में पोर्टेबिलिटी के लिए कितने दिन पहले आवेदन करना होगा?
results.expected_answer: पोर्टेबिलिटी के लिए नवीनीकरण की तारीख से कम से कम 45 दिन पहले आवेदन करना होगा।
results.actual_answer: I don't have enough information to answer this reliably.

results.question: UPI पर प्रति लेनदेन अधिकतम सीमा क्या है?
results.expected_answer: UPI पर प्रति लेनदेन अधिकतम राशि 1,00,000 रुपये है।
